Cedar Review - Empires & Puzzles 3-Star Holy Hero Strategy Guide

Is Cedar the accuracy-debuffing champion your 3-star roster needs, or just another primate waiting to be benched? We analyze his chain-swinging skills, his stats in the face of modern power creep, and where he might still find a home in your team.
Cedar Review: The Monkey King's Mini-Me or Just Monkeying Around?
Cedar: The Chain-Swinging Simian Bringing Misses to the Masses

Cedar is a 3-star Holy Ranger belonging to the Jungle Hunter family, released back in the wild days of mid-2023. Visually reminiscent of a certain legendary Monkey King, Cedar brings a chaotic mix of AoE damage and accuracy manipulation to the 3-star arena. While his raw damage output might feel a bit like a "soft boop" compared to the earth-shattering hits of modern heroes, his utility—specifically forcing enemies to miss their shots—can be the difference between a win and a wipeout. He doesn't have a costume, so what you see is what you get: a frantic monkey with a chain and a plan.

Cedar’s artwork is dynamic and full of motion, capturing him mid-swing with his "Titanbone Chain." The vibrant jungle greens and his determined (if slightly manic) expression give him a distinct personality. He looks like he’s having the time of his life, which is more than we can say for the enemies missing their specials because of him. It’s a solid design that fits perfectly within the "Untold Tales" aesthetic—wild, untamed, and a little bit dangerous.
When we look at Cedar's stats through the lens of 2026, the picture is... a bit dusty.

These are his weak points. With modern "Glass" family heroes and Constructs boasting nearly 1000 HP and higher defense, Cedar is incredibly fragile. He is effectively a "glass cannon" without the cannon-level firepower. He needs protection or emblems to survive more than a stiff breeze.
is a mixed bag of "meh" damage and "wow" utility.
175% to all enemies is weak. In today's meta, this barely scratches enemies with high defense or boosted health. Do not rely on Cedar as your primary kill-shot.
The Debuff (The Star of the Show):

The accuracy debuff is where Cedar earns his bananas. Starting at -15% and potentially dropping to -35% if the enemies are hit repeatedly is a potent disruption tool. In 3-star tournaments, causing a key sniper or healer to miss can completely swing the match.

The +20% attack stack against the weaker element (Dark) is a nice bonus, especially against the ubiquitous Treevil tanks in Rush tournaments, but it takes time to build up—time Cedar might not have given his fragility.

Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Jungle Hunter family brings some interesting mechanics to the table that help patch up Cedar's weaknesses.
The Jungle Hunter bonus is generally offensive, but requires multiple family members to really shine. Alone, it doesn't offer Cedar much, but if paired with other Hunters, the synergy can be decent.
As mentioned, this adds a DoT component to his special. It's free damage on a random target, which is always welcome.
+20% Attack for 3 turns on cast helps his subsequent tile damage or slashes, trying to compensate for his lower base attack stat.
is a simple, effective boost. It helps him deal a bit more damage, but given his utility role, a defensive Aether Power might have been more beneficial to keep him alive longer.
Upon release, Cedar was seen as an interesting utility hero—accuracy down is rare in 3-star rarities. He wasn't a meta-breaker like Treevil, but he was a solid option for depth.

The game has moved fast. New 3-star heroes from the "Construct", "Astral", and "Glass" families have stats that eclipse Cedar's. A modern 3-star tank can have stats comparable to old 4-stars. Cedar's 477 Defense makes him a liability against these new powerhouses.
. He is not a starter for a general defense team. His viability is almost entirely restricted to
scenarios where his accuracy debuff can mitigate incoming damage since there is no healing.

Currently, Cedar does not have a costume. This puts him at a further disadvantage regarding stats compared to the many "Toon" and "Costume 3" versions of classic heroes running around with massive stat bonuses.
Cedar is squishy. Very squishy. You have two main schools of thought here, but for him to do his job (debuffing), he needs to stay alive.
Keeps him alive long enough to cast his special at least once. Maximizes the value of his accuracy debuff by keeping him on the field.

He will likely die to a stiff breeze or a single slash from a modern sniper before charging.
path is recommended. Cedar is a support hero disguised as a ranger; his value is in the miss chance, not the kill count.
Causing enemies to miss is a powerful form of mitigation that doesn't rely on defense stats.
The stack bonus against Dark enemies makes him a specific counter-pick against heavy Dark teams (looking at you, Rush Tournament Treevil tanks).
His bulk is dangerously low for the current meta.

If you pulled Cedar early, he is a solid 3-star to level. He will help you survive tougher map stages by making bosses miss. Emblem him for defense so he doesn't die on you.

Cedar is likely a depth piece for you. He’s worth keeping for Rush Wars or Tournaments if you lack other strong Yellow options, but don't prioritize Limit Breaking him over modern meta heroes like the Toons or Constructs unless you really enjoy his playstyle. He is
The community reception to Cedar was mixed but generally positive about his utility.
"His hit of 175% (even if it’s to all) is quite low and weak. But his accuracy reduction is worth gold." -
A common sentiment comparing him to the 5-star Justice.
Many players noted that while his damage is lackluster, "the +20% against dark is fun to take down Treevil / Greel combinations."

Cedar is a fun, chaotic hero who brings a unique flavor of disruption to the 3-star tier. He isn't a powerhouse who will wipe teams alone, but he is the banana peel on the floor that makes the enemy slip up just when they thought they had won. While his stats are showing their age, his ability to force misses keeps him relevant in specific niches.
A solid B-tier hero for tournaments, mostly kept around for his ability to make opponents look foolish.
A comprehensive review of Cedar, the 3-Star Holy Ranger from the Jungle Hunter family. We analyze his accuracy debuff, stacking mechanics, and viability in the current Empires & Puzzles meta.
